zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Scales IV 1st Baron de Scales於大約1249年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可米德萊頓時，他的父親Robert de Scales II32歲，母親Clementia28歲。他於1278年之前與Muriel de Liscuris在英國英格蘭諾福爾可結婚。他去世於1305年9月4日之前在英國英格蘭諾福爾可米德萊頓，埋葬在英國諾福爾可布拉可波洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert de Scales IV 1st Baron de Scales于大约1249年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可米德萊頓时，他的父亲Robert de Scales II32岁，母亲Clementia28岁。他于1278年之前与Muriel de Liscuris在英国英格兰諾福爾可结婚。他去世于1305年9月4日之前在英国英格兰諾福爾可米德萊頓，埋葬在英国諾福爾可布拉可波洛。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Scales
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: plural form of   Scale  , naming a herdsman living and working on a summer pasture, or a habitational name for someone from a place called Scales, mostly minor places in Lancashire and Yorkshire.   Scholes   is a dialect variant of the same name.

(of Norman origin): habitational name either from Écalles-Alix (Seine-Maritime), Écalles (in Estouteville-Écalles, Seine-Maritime), or from Escalles (Pas-de-Calais). The earliest spellings of these names suggest a derivation from Latin scala, scalae (plural) ‘ladder, staircase, stile’, perhaps applied to a steep slope or path, although Old Norse skáli (‘herdsman's hut, shieling’, see Scale ) has also been suggested for Écalles. Challis may be a variant of the same name. The surname has also been established in southern Ireland (Limerick) since the 14th century.

variants.name: Scholes
meanings: English: habitational name from one or other of the places called Scholes in Lancashire and Yorkshire, or from Scole in Norfolk, all named with Middle English scole or scoles ‘herdsman's hut(s)’ (Old Norse skáli). In northern England the name was sometimes interchangeable with Scales , a dialect variant of the same word.

variants.name: Scale
meanings: English: from northern Middle English scale ‘herdsman's hut, shed, shieling’ (Old Norse skáli). The surname may have denoted a herdsman who lived and worked on a summer pasture or someone from any of several mostly minor places so named in Lancashire and Yorkshire. The name often varies between singular and plural forms; see Scales .

variants.name: Schooley
meanings: English (Sussex): variant of Scholey, a habitational name either from Scholey's Bridge in Hemsworth (Yorkshire) or the lost Scholey in Rastrick (Yorkshire), recorded in that spelling in 1399. The placenames derive from Old Norse skáli ‘(temporary) hut, shed, shieling’ + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Compare Scales .

variants.name: Schoolcraft
meanings: English: variant of Scowcroft, from Middle English scale, sc(h)ole ‘hut, shed’ (Old Norse skáli) + croft ‘small enclosed field’. The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a field containing a hut, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Scowcroft in Oldham (Lancashire) and Scholecroft in Morley (Yorkshire). Compare Scales and Schofield .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Scales IV 1st Baron de Scales was born about 1249, in Middleton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert de Scales II, was 32 and his mother, Clementia, was 28. He married Muriel de Liscuris before 1278, in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. He died before 4 September 1305, in Middleton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Blackborough, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Scales IV 1st Baron de Scales est né environ 1249, à Middleton, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de Scales II, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Clementia, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Muriel de Liscuris avant 1278, à Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé avant 4 septembre 1305, à Middleton, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Blackborough, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Scales IV 1st Baron de Scales um 1249 in Middleton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Scales II 32 und seine Mutter Clementia 28. Er hat vor 1278 in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Muriel de Liscuris geheiratet. Er starb vor 4. September 1305 in Middleton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Blackborough, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Scales IV 1st Baron de Scales è nato il circa 1249 nel Middleton, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Robert de Scales II, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Clementia, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Muriel de Liscuris prima del 1278, nel Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì prima del 4 settembre 1305, nel Middleton, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolto a Blackborough, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Scales IV 1st Baron de Scalesが約1249年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクミッドレトンで生まれた時，父Robert de Scales IIは32歳，母Clementiaは28歳であった。1278年以前にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクでMuriel de Liscurisと結婚した。彼は1305年9月4日以前にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクミッドレトンで死去し，イギリスノーフォルクブラクボロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Scales IV 1st Baron de Scales(이)가 대략 1249년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 미들레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Scales II(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Clementia(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1278년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크에서 Muriel de Liscuris(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1305년 9월 4일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 미들레턴에서 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 블랙버루에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Scales IV 1st Baron de Scales nasceu aproximadamente 1249, em Middleton, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert de Scales II, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Clementia, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Muriel de Liscuris antes 1278, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu antes 4 de setembro de 1305, em Middleton, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultado em Blackborough, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Scales IV 1st Baron de Scales родился около 1249 г. в местности миддлетон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert de Scales II было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Clementia, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Muriel de Liscuris перед 1278 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер перед 4 сентябрь 1305 г. в местности миддлетон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности блэккбороую, норфолк, Англия.
